projects.title: AI & Marketing Automation
projects.short: Building AI-powered internal tools and personal products — from auto-updating dashboards to email automation and app prototypes.

items: I use AI coding tools (Claude Code, Claude Design) to build marketing automation: internal tools that remove manual work and personal products I ship end-to-end.
Internal tools: a campaign landing page, an auto-updating data dashboard and automated email workflows for the K-Tech campaign.
Personal products: mobile app concepts prototyped with AI coding tools.
A mobile app concept built with AI coding tools. [I will provide details]
Built and shipped a campaign landing page with Claude Code + Claude Design, deployed via GitHub + Vercel.
Purpose: give candidates a single, clear entry point to learn about the program and register.
Outcome: reduced candidate confusion and gave the team a fast, reliable page they could update anytime.
Consolidated scattered Google Sheets into one auto-updating dashboard (Claude + GitHub + Vercel).
Gave the team a single source of truth for tracking and analysis.
Saved significant manual reporting time each week.
Automated email workflows with Google Apps Script.
Trigger logic: emails sent automatically based on form submissions and status changes.
Time saved: cut repetitive manual sending while improving response consistency.

projects.title: K-Tech College — Awareness & Lead Campaign
projects.short: Built awareness and drove 5K+ qualified leads for an international tech education program through a digital-first, community-driven campaign.

items: Planned and executed a full-funnel campaign (Awareness → Consideration → Conversion) to promote an international tech education program to Vietnam's IT community.
Combined social media, Meta Ads, landing page optimization, third-party job boards and offline tech events into one integrated strategy.
Low awareness of the program in Vietnam's tech community.
Needed to reach two very different audiences: students and working developers.
Limited campaign timeline demanded fast testing and optimization.
Contributed 33% of total registrations via Meta Ads while successfully reducing CPL.
KTC Hackathon 2025 reached 174K+ impressions, 129K+ engagements and 5K+ leads.
Achieved high satisfaction scores and positive feedback on candidate support and experience.
→ See the AI tools I built for this campaign
Meta Ads: tested 3+ ad angles and multiple formats (carousel, video, single image), refined copy per audience segment to improve CTR and lower cost per application.
Promoted job posts on LinkedIn to reach working developers with targeted placements. [I will provide details]
Partnerships: coordinated with ITviec and VietnamWorks for media placements reaching targeted IT audiences.
Social media: planned Facebook & LinkedIn content across 4 pillars — career opportunities, program introduction, social proof, event promotion.
Nurtured the developer community through group engagement and conversation. [I will provide details]
Seeded the program across relevant forums, groups and communities. [I will provide details]
Landing page & Notion job page: proposed content edits that reduced candidate confusion, and built a Notion job page that helped candidates find roles while saving project costs.
Published organic job listings on hiring platforms to reach candidates for free. [I will provide details]
Partnered with universities to reach students through campus channels. [I will provide details]
Tech events: developed concepts, agendas and judging criteria for K-TECH FEST, K-TECH NEXUS and KTC HACKATHON; acted as primary contact for all participants.
Ran online events and webinars to engage remote audiences. [I will provide details]

projects.title: Copywriter Projects
projects.short: Strategic messaging, big ideas and content execution for clients across tech, F&B, education and energy.

items: As a copywriter at WEALLNET Agency, developed messaging strategy and creative concepts for featured clients: imoo Watch Phone, FROMCAUDAT Coffee, VITASA Academy and Ha Thien Energy.
Conducted market research, competitor analysis and industry trend evaluation for every brief.
Analyzed target customer personas and developed key messages with a brand-appropriate tone of voice.
Each client came from a different industry with distinct audiences — from parents buying kids' smartwatches to takeaway coffee cart owners.
Messaging had to balance brand-appropriate tone of voice with concrete conversion goals.
Received positive client feedback on proposals — approved for next steps.
Delivered full messaging frameworks (Get who / To do what / By how) ready for media rollout.
imoo Z7: positioned the smartwatch as peace-of-mind for busy parents (kids 5–12), with content built on safety, real-life stories and product comparisons across Google, Facebook Ads and website UX.
FROMCAUDAT: crafted a community-driven narrative ('Nhà Đồng Hành') to scale the takeaway coffee franchise toward 750 carts in 10 months, targeting sellers, student baristas and busy professionals.

projects.title: Social Media
projects.short: Grew community and brand pages to 26.7M views and +15.1K followers, and scripted trend-driven short videos including a 9.9M-view viral hit.

items: Managed three Facebook pages under WEALLNET: Cong Dong Da Sac (LGBT+ community page), WAN Creative Network (agency brand page) and WANTalk (marketing knowledge hub).
Developed video ideas and scripts inspired by TikTok trends and real customer/brand stories, across fashion & beauty, humor & entertainment, food & beverage, and horror.
Each page needed its own voice: humorous memes for a community, professional insight for a brand, and educational content for learners.
Growth had to come primarily from organic, shareable content — not ad budget.
Researched trends and audience behavior to plan, propose and execute monthly content strategies.
Created memes, posts and videos; designed visuals; engaged with the community and managed comments daily.
Tracked performance and continuously adjusted growth strategy based on data.
Cong Dong Da Sac: 26.7M views, 7.3M reach and +15.1K followers in 4 months, with multiple viral posts (3.2M / 2.8M / 2.7M / 2.6M views).
WAN Creative Network: 248.5K views, 140.5K users reached (+27.2%), 41% organic traffic.
WANTalk: 15.9K views in 4 months with 70% organic traffic.
Trends on TikTok expire in days — scripts had to be produced fast while staying entertaining, clear and TikTok-compliant.
Each script needed to fit the target audience and brand goals without losing the hook.
Stayed on top of viral memes and formats, adapting them to each brand's voice.
Tailored scripts to specific audiences (youth, comedy, product storytelling) and aligned video style with marketing goals.
Scripted a viral video with 9.9M+ views and 242K+ likes.
Maintained a video series averaging 50K–100K views per video.
Content was widely shared, growing followers and engagement organically.

projects.title: Other
projects.short: University and self-driven projects — from leading a campus design competition to building a full brand identity system.

items: A collection of university and self-driven projects showcasing leadership, project management and brand-building skills — including the Cre8Vibe logo design competition and the Brown Delight chocolate branding project.
Leader of Cre8Vibe — a logo design competition at HUFLIT — owning the entire organization process from concept to post-event evaluation.
Full ownership: objectives, concept, timeline, budget, team assignment, partner coordination and issue handling.
Needed to attract participants in a crowded student-event landscape.
Defined objectives, concept, timeline and budget; assigned tasks and supervised the team.
Built the full communication plan — content, media design and promotion — to drive registrations.
Designed competition rules, judging criteria, and scripts for MCs and guest trainers.
Grew the fanpage by 400 followers in two weeks with 3–11% engagement throughout.
Kept the event within budget, cutting projected costs by 15%, with 90% positive participant feedback.

Brown Delight is a branding project for a Vietnamese chocolate brand aiming for the global market — chocolate infused with local specialty fruits, and packaging inspired by Vietnamese cultural heritage.
Build a cohesive brand from zero: concept, brand key, positioning, identity system and marketing program — all aligned with one cultural story.
Led the project end-to-end, overseeing strategy, execution and creative direction.
Researched and executed brand concept, brand key and brand positioning.
Led creative development of the Brand Identity System, including packaging featuring Vietnamese cultural architecture and Dak Lak cocoa origin.
Proposed and planned a Brand Marketing Program aligned with the set objectives.
Achieved a score of 8.5/10 with positive feedback from the instructor.
Successfully implemented a structured, cohesive branding process from concept to marketing plan.
